impounded_tanker.title: THE IMPOUNDED TANKER
impounded_tanker.description: An Iranian Revolutionary Guard fast-boat swarm harassed a commercial tanker three days ago. The crew is safe but the ship is impounded in Bandar Abbas. Your predecessor resigned in disgrace. You are the replacement. Nobody expects you to last.

choices.label: Demand release through official channels
choices.flavor: You follow process. The world sees restraint. Iran sees an opening.

choices.label: Send a destroyer to loiter outside Bandar Abbas
choices.flavor: You say nothing. The image speaks. IRGC commanders take note.

choices.label: Call the flag-state directly — propose a joint response
choices.flavor: Shared burden, shared credit. The coalition option opens.

characterFlavor.trump: Another crisis for the media to obsess over — but crises are ratings, and ratings are power.
characterFlavor.hegseth: IRGC fast-boats operating with impunity against commercial shipping — this demands a proportional military response.
characterFlavor.kushner: The seizure creates leverage with Gulf partners who need protection — time to call MBS.
characterFlavor.fuentes: An American-crewed ship seized because we're policing waters six thousand miles from home. Bring them home.
characterFlavor.asmongold: Chat, this is exactly the scenario I flagged in my Day 1 analysis — if my prediction holds, credibility surges.

predecessors_ghost.title: THE PREDECESSOR'S GHOST
predecessors_ghost.description: A classified folder sits on your desk — your predecessor's secret negotiations with Tehran. Reading it is knowledge. Burning it is a clean start. The contents are radioactive either way.

choices.label: Read the classified file
choices.flavor: You now know Iran's real red line. This knowledge will matter — if it doesn't destroy you first.

choices.label: Burn the file — start clean
choices.flavor: The public trusts a clean break. But you'll discover Iran's red line the hard way — through a crisis.

admirals_warning.title: THE ADMIRAL'S WARNING
admirals_warning.description: Your first real briefing. Admiral Chen stands at the podium with satellite imagery of Iranian naval movements. "We need to increase patrol tempo immediately," he says. Your civilian advisors shift uncomfortably.

choices.label: Defer to the Admiral — increase patrols
choices.flavor: The Admiral nods. Your civilian advisors resent being sidelined. The Pentagon respects the energy.

choices.label: Convene your full team before deciding
choices.flavor: The Admiral respects the process. Barely. Your team appreciates being heard.

choices.label: Override — propose a diplomatic signal instead
choices.flavor: The Admiral begins quietly briefing Congress about your "lack of seriousness." But Araghchi notices.

journalists_call.title: THE JOURNALIST'S CALL
journalists_call.description: A New York Times reporter is on the line. She's working on a piece about your first 72 hours. Off-the-record, she says. But you know how that goes.

choices.label: Give an off-the-record briefing
choices.flavor: You shape the narrative. But there's a 20% chance she burns you.

choices.label: Written statement through press office
choices.flavor: "Administration stonewalling" runs above the fold. Safe but unhelpful.

choices.label: "No comment" — hang up
choices.flavor: She becomes hostile. Future media events will be harder.

british_pm_call.title: ALLY ON THE LINE: THE BRITISH PM
british_pm_call.description: The British Prime Minister is offering a joint naval task force. Shared command, shared rules of engagement. It's generous — suspiciously so. Nothing is free in the special relationship.

choices.label: Accept — joint task force
choices.flavor: Shared command means shared veto. You lose unilateral strike capability. But the coalition is real.

choices.label: Counter with parallel operations — same area, separate commands
choices.flavor: You keep your freedom. The UK is mildly insulted but professional.

choices.label: Ask what they want in return
choices.flavor: The PM wants your vote on a UN trade resolution. Opens a side-negotiation.

first_seizure_attempt.title: THE FIRST SEIZURE ATTEMPT
first_seizure_attempt.description: FLASH traffic from CENTCOM: IRGC fast-boats converging on the MV Pacific Meridian, a Liberian-flagged tanker carrying Japanese electronics. This is the game's first real test.

choices.label: Scramble jets — verbal warning over radio
choices.flavor: 70% chance the seizure is deterred. Standard procedure. If it fails, you own the failure.

choices.label: Authorize warning shots across the bow
choices.flavor: The world watches nervously. 90% chance Iran backs down. But escalation is real.

choices.label: Let it happen — document everything for the UN
choices.flavor: The tanker is seized. It's painful. But the footage at the UN will be devastating for Iran.

characterFlavor.trump: If we stop the seizure, it's the greatest naval interception in history. If we don't, the media eats us alive.
characterFlavor.hegseth: This is a direct provocation against commercial shipping under US Navy protection. Rules of engagement are clear — respond with force.
characterFlavor.kushner: A seizure attempt changes the insurance calculus — Gulf states will pay premium for protection arrangements.
characterFlavor.fuentes: They're attacking tankers because American forces are still there to shoot at. No presence, no provocation.
characterFlavor.asmongold: Real-time seizure attempt on stream — chat is going absolutely insane. This is the content that makes or breaks a prediction.

oil_markets_panic.title: OIL MARKETS PANIC
oil_markets_panic.description: Oil futures jumped $15/barrel overnight. The Energy Secretary is on the phone. Gas prices are on every cable news chyron. The Saudi Energy Minister might increase production — if you ask nicely.

choices.label: Release strategic petroleum reserves
choices.flavor: Markets calm immediately. But reserves are finite — you've bought 10 days, not a solution.

choices.label: Call Saudi — beg for increased production
choices.flavor: MBS agrees — for a price you'll pay later. The humiliating leak comes on Day 20.

choices.label: Let markets find their own level
choices.flavor: Pain now, stability later. The opposition runs "gas prices" attack ads within hours.

un_showdown.title: UN SECURITY COUNCIL SHOWDOWN
un_showdown.description: The Security Council is in emergency session. You have one shot to get a binding resolution. Russia is leaning veto. China is undecided. Your ambassador is waiting for instructions.

choices.label: Push for binding resolution with sanctions
choices.flavor: Russia abstains. China follows. The resolution passes. Iran is formally isolated.

choices.label: Non-binding statement of concern — play it safe
choices.flavor: Safe, symbolic, useless. Your team is demoralized.

choices.label: Dramatic intelligence presentation — name IRGC commanders
choices.flavor: The room is shocked. Iran scrambles. But you just burned intelligence sources.

proxy_ignition.title: PROXY IGNITION: HOUTHI STRIKE
proxy_ignition.description: Houthis launched anti-ship missiles at a commercial vessel in the Red Sea. The crisis is no longer contained to the strait — it's metastasizing. Your budget is burning and allies are nervous.

choices.label: Strike the Houthi launch site
choices.flavor: Proportional. Professional. Saudi Arabia is grateful. But you just opened a second front.

choices.label: Intercept missiles — let proxies tire themselves out
choices.flavor: Defense only. The Houthis try again in 3 days.

choices.label: Tell Iran through channels: proxy attacks = Tehran's responsibility
choices.flavor: A red line. If Iran believes you, this changes everything. If they don't, it changes nothing.

the_leak.title: THE LEAK
the_leak.description: The Washington Post has your classified strategy memo. Every detail of your Iran approach is now public. Someone in your inner circle betrayed you. The press is circling.

choices.label: Launch an aggressive investigation — find the leaker
choices.flavor: You burn political capital hunting the mole. If you find them, credibility skyrockets.

choices.label: Spin it — frame the leak as showing your brilliant strategy
choices.flavor: Bold. Iran now knows your playbook. But your base thinks you're playing 4D chess.

choices.label: Ignore it — refuse to dignify leaks
choices.flavor: The news cycle moves on. But your staff knows there are no consequences for betrayal.

congressional_hearing_big.title: THE CONGRESSIONAL HEARING
congressional_hearing_big.description: Senate Armed Services Committee. Live on C-SPAN. The chairman opens with: "The American people deserve to know why we're spending $50 million a day on a crisis that's getting worse." The cameras are rolling.

choices.label: Testify personally — face the music
choices.flavor: You came prepared. The viral clip is you calmly dismantling the chairman's argument with classified data.

choices.label: Send your deputy — you're managing a crisis
choices.flavor: Congress is insulted. Future funding requests just got harder.

choices.label: Testify AND declassify satellite imagery live on camera
choices.flavor: The room gasps. Iran scrambles. The Pentagon is furious about the declassification. But the public is with you.

characterFlavor.trump: Congress is TV, and TV is my medium. Three hours of free airtime to control the narrative.
characterFlavor.hegseth: The Armed Services Committee wants operational briefings — fine. The military record speaks for itself.
characterFlavor.kushner: Congressional hearings expose back-channel work. Every minute on camera is a minute of exposure risk.
characterFlavor.fuentes: The same people who voted for every war since 2001 are now lecturing me about spending. The base sees the hypocrisy.
characterFlavor.asmongold: Congressional hearing on stream — this is S-tier content. Chat's engagement metrics will be through the roof.

iran_ultimatum.title: IRAN'S ULTIMATUM
iran_ultimatum.description: Mojtaba Khamenei appears on state television: "Lift sanctions on our central bank within 72 hours, or the next vessel we seize will fly the American flag." Tangsiri stands behind him, arms crossed. The clock is ticking.

choices.label: Call the bluff publicly — "America does not respond to threats"
choices.flavor: If Iran follows through, this gets catastrophic. If they back down, you win the crisis.

choices.label: Counter-propose through channels — mutual step-down
choices.flavor: 50% Iran accepts. Araghchi is pushing for it internally. Tangsiri wants blood.

choices.label: Prepare quietly — move assets, brief allies, say nothing
choices.flavor: Iran is uncertain. Their own internal debate intensifies. Sometimes silence is the loudest response.

characterFlavor.trump: Iran just gave us the greatest TV moment since 'Mission Accomplished.' The question is how we spin the response.
characterFlavor.hegseth: Ultimatums require military credibility to back them up. Our force posture needs to be unambiguous.
characterFlavor.kushner: An ultimatum from Tehran opens a negotiation window — they wouldn't issue demands if they didn't want a deal.
characterFlavor.fuentes: Iran is desperate because sanctions are working. We don't need to escalate — we need to leave and let the pressure do its job.
characterFlavor.asmongold: Seventy-two hour timer just started, chat. This is the boss fight. Everything I've predicted about Iran's strategy is about to be tested.

intel_breakthrough.title: THE INTELLIGENCE BREAKTHROUGH
intel_breakthrough.description: A SIGINT intercept combined with a HUMINT source has revealed Iran's true strategic objective. This isn't about the strait — it's about forcing recognition of Iran's regional hegemony. Every move they've made was building toward this moment. The question is what you do with the knowledge.

choices.label: Share with allies — build a coalition response
choices.flavor: Allies are galvanized. But your intelligence methods are exposed. Future intel quality degrades.

choices.label: Keep it classified — act unilaterally
choices.flavor: You know something nobody else does. That's power. But allies will feel blindsided later.

choices.label: Leak to a trusted journalist — shape the narrative
choices.flavor: The public needs to know. Iran changes plans. The intelligence becomes partly obsolete. But public pressure on Iran surges.

characterFlavor.trump: This is the kind of intelligence win that plays beautifully on camera — if we can declassify the right parts.
characterFlavor.hegseth: Actionable intelligence on Iran's true objectives — this changes the entire force planning calculus.
characterFlavor.kushner: Understanding Iran's real endgame means understanding what they'll trade for. This intel is deal-making gold.
characterFlavor.fuentes: So Iran's real goal was never the strait — it was regional hegemony. More proof that staying in the Middle East serves THEIR interests, not ours.
characterFlavor.asmongold: Dude. The intel just confirmed what I said on stream three days ago. If chat clipped that prediction, my credibility goes to the moon.

journalist_returns.title: THE JOURNALIST RETURNS
journalist_returns.description: Remember the Times reporter? She's back. This time she wants to be embedded with your team for 48 hours. Radical transparency — or radical exposure. Your relationship with her determines how this plays out.

choices.label: Full on-the-record interview — you've earned it
choices.flavor: Your narrative is coherent. The piece is fair. For once, the press is on your side.

choices.label: Embed her with your team for 48 hours
choices.flavor: She sees everything — including your mistakes. But the transparency is powerful. 70% chance of a glowing profile.

choices.label: Feed her a specific story you want published
choices.flavor: It works — this time. But journalists have long memories about being used.

carrier_incident.title: THE CARRIER INCIDENT
carrier_incident.description: An Iranian drone buzzed the USS Eisenhower's flight deck at 50 feet. Sailors hit the deck. Gun cameras captured everything. CENTCOM wants to release the footage. Iran claims it was "routine surveillance."

choices.label: Issue formal protest through military channels
choices.flavor: Professionals talk to professionals. Iran absorbs the protest. IRGC internally: "They flinched."

choices.label: Declassify footage — release to CNN
choices.flavor: The footage goes viral. Iran is embarrassed globally. But Tangsiri remembers the humiliation.

choices.label: Retaliate — disable the drone command facility
choices.flavor: Precision strike. Clean hit. The world holds its breath. Iran goes silent for 48 hours.

secret_talks.title: SECRET BACK-CHANNEL
secret_talks.description: Iranian moderates have reached out through Omani intermediaries. They propose secret talks — but if leaked, hawks on both sides will be furious.

choices.label: Accept the talks
choices.flavor: The back-channel opens. Both sides agree to a cooling-off period.

choices.label: Reject — too risky
choices.flavor: The opportunity passes. Iranian hardliners use the rejection as propaganda.

choices.label: Leak it to the press
choices.flavor: You gain public credit but burn the diplomatic bridge.

congress_pressure.title: CONGRESSIONAL HEARING
congress_pressure.description: Senate Armed Services Committee demands testimony. They threaten to cut your budget.

choices.label: Justify with intelligence
choices.flavor: You declassify key intel. Congress is satisfied — for now.

choices.label: Promise to cut spending
choices.flavor: Congress relents but expects follow-through.

choices.label: Stonewall the committee
choices.flavor: Refusing backfires. Media coverage turns hostile.

allied_request.title: ALLIED NAVAL REQUEST
allied_request.description: Japan requests dedicated US Navy escort for their tanker fleet. It strengthens the alliance but costs resources.

choices.label: Assign escorts
choices.flavor: Japanese tankers sail with USN escorts. Tokyo publicly thanks Washington.

choices.label: Decline politely
choices.flavor: Japan is disappointed but understanding.

humanitarian.title: HUMANITARIAN CRISIS
humanitarian.description: An Iranian fishing vessel capsized near the strait. 40 fishermen in the water. Your destroyer is 15 minutes away.

choices.label: Rescue immediately
choices.flavor: Your sailors pull 38 survivors from the water. The footage goes viral.

choices.label: Coordinate with Iran
choices.flavor: Joint effort saves most of the crew.

choices.label: Stay on mission
choices.flavor: Iranian state media reports US ships watched fishermen drown.

media_crisis.title: MEDIA FIRESTORM
media_crisis.description: Footage of a near-collision between US and Iranian vessels. Cable news running it 24/7.

choices.label: Release full video
choices.flavor: Unedited footage shows Iranian provocation. Narrative shifts in your favor.

choices.label: Downplay the incident
choices.flavor: The story fades. Both sides quietly move on.

choices.label: Blame Iran publicly
choices.flavor: Forceful condemnation rallies base but inflames Iran.

intel_reveal.title: INTELLIGENCE BREAKTHROUGH
intel_reveal.description: Your agents located Iran's mine-laying operations. Acting on it would burn the source.

choices.label: Strike the mine depot
choices.flavor: Cruise missiles destroy the depot. Mine threat neutralized.

choices.label: Share with allies quietly
choices.flavor: Coalition partners mine-sweep the areas. Source stays safe.

choices.label: Hold the intelligence
choices.flavor: You file it for future use.

china_mediation.title: CHINESE MEDIATION OFFER
china_mediation.description: Beijing proposes brokering a deal: they'll pressure Iran if you ease tech restrictions on Chinese firms.

choices.label: Accept the deal
choices.flavor: China delivers. Iran pulls back. But tech concessions draw criticism.

choices.label: Counter-propose
choices.flavor: Negotiations drag on. Modest gains.

choices.label: Reject outright
choices.flavor: Hawks applaud. China is annoyed.

houthi_attack.title: HOUTHI RED SEA ATTACK
houthi_attack.description: Houthi forces have struck a commercial vessel in the Red Sea with an anti-ship missile. Iran's proxy war is expanding.

choices.label: Strike Houthi launch sites
choices.flavor: Precision strikes destroy launch infrastructure. Houthis go quiet briefly.

choices.label: Ask Saudi Arabia to handle it
choices.flavor: Riyadh engages. Results are mixed but it shares the burden.

choices.label: Condemn and do nothing
choices.flavor: Critics ask why you're not responding to attacks on global shipping.

militia_attack.title: MILITIA BASE ATTACK
militia_attack.description: Iraqi militia rockets hit a US base in the region. Two soldiers wounded. CENTCOM wants authorization to retaliate.

choices.label: Retaliatory strike
choices.flavor: Precision strikes hit militia warehouses. A clear message sent.

choices.label: Increase base security
choices.flavor: You absorb the attack. Some call it restraint, others weakness.

choices.label: Withdraw from the base
choices.flavor: The withdrawal is spun as a retreat. Iran claims victory.

assassination_intel.title: CREDIBLE THREAT DETECTED
assassination_intel.description: Intelligence agencies detect a credible assassination threat against senior officials. The source is unclear — could be Iranian or domestic.

choices.label: Increase security protocols
choices.flavor: Security is tightened. Operations slow but the threat is contained.

choices.label: Public announcement
choices.flavor: Going public rallies support but raises tensions.

choices.label: Ignore — focus on the mission
choices.flavor: You choose to focus on the strait. The risk remains.

domestic_unrest.title: DOMESTIC UNREST
domestic_unrest.description: Protests erupt in major cities over war spending and the strait crisis. The National Guard is requesting authorization.

choices.label: Address the nation
choices.flavor: Your speech calls for unity. Polls stabilize temporarily.

choices.label: Deploy National Guard
choices.flavor: The crackdown makes headlines worldwide. Things get worse.

choices.label: Announce peace initiative
choices.flavor: A bold move. Moderates applaud, hawks protest.

hostage.title: HOSTAGE SITUATION
hostage.description: Iran is holding 12 crew members from a seized tanker. Families are on every news channel.

choices.label: Negotiate release
choices.flavor: You open a channel through Swiss intermediaries. The crew is alive but scared.

choices.label: Demand unconditional release
choices.flavor: You increase pressure. International opinion turns against Iran.

choices.label: Offer quiet concession
choices.flavor: A minor sanctions waiver. The crew comes home. Nobody talks about the price.

oil_spike.title: OIL MARKET PANIC
oil_spike.description: Oil futures jumped $15/barrel. Energy Secretary is on the phone — should you release reserves?

choices.label: Release reserves
choices.flavor: SPR release calms markets. Prices stabilize.

choices.label: Verbal intervention only
choices.flavor: Your press conference helps a little. Markets remain jittery.

choices.label: Let markets correct
choices.flavor: Gas prices soar. Critics call you out of touch.

gulf_offer.title: GULF STATE OFFER
gulf_offer.description: Saudi Arabia and UAE offer to fund 60% of your operations — but they want a say in rules of engagement.

choices.label: Accept with conditions
choices.flavor: Gulf funding flows in. You retain operational control.

choices.label: Accept fully
choices.flavor: Generous funding helps but critics question who's calling the shots.

choices.label: Decline
choices.flavor: You maintain independence but miss burden-sharing.

drone_shootdown.title: DRONE SHOOT-DOWN
drone_shootdown.description: Iran shot down your surveillance drone over international waters. Joint Chiefs want a proportional response.

choices.label: Strike the missile battery
choices.flavor: Precision strike destroys the SAM site. The world holds its breath.

choices.label: Respond with more drones
choices.flavor: You flood the area with drones. Iran doesn't dare shoot again.

choices.label: Stand down — it was unmanned
choices.flavor: You absorb the loss. Some call it wise, others call it weak.

un_vote.title: UN RESOLUTION VOTE
un_vote.description: A resolution condemning Iran is up for vote. Russia will veto unless you water it down.

choices.label: Push strong resolution
choices.flavor: Russia vetoes. But the debate isolates Iran diplomatically.

choices.label: Accept watered-down version
choices.flavor: Passes unanimously. Symbolic but shows unity.

choices.label: Withdraw the resolution
choices.flavor: Allies are confused by the retreat.

cyber_attack_decision.title: CYBER OPERATION PROPOSAL
cyber_attack_decision.description: NSA can disable Iran's naval command network for 48 hours. If attributed, it's an act of war.

choices.label: Approve the operation
choices.flavor: Iran's boats go silent for two days. They suspect sabotage.

choices.label: Too dangerous — deny
choices.flavor: You shelve the plan. Better to save that card.

election_pressure.title: ELECTION YEAR PRESSURE
election_pressure.description: Your party is down in polls. Advisors want a dramatic move before midterms.

choices.label: Stage a naval exercise
choices.flavor: Carriers look great on camera. Polls bump. Iran calls it provocation.

choices.label: Focus on diplomacy
choices.flavor: Not flashy. Pundits debate whether it's leadership or weakness.

choices.label: Ignore the advisors
choices.flavor: You stay the course. The polls are what they are.

pipeline_sabotage.title: PIPELINE SABOTAGE
pipeline_sabotage.description: An underwater pipeline feeding a major terminal has been damaged. Could be Iran, could be an accident.

choices.label: Blame Iran, escalate
choices.flavor: The accusation rallies allies but stokes the fire.

choices.label: Investigate first
choices.flavor: Measured response. The investigation takes time.

choices.label: Offer joint investigation
choices.flavor: Surprisingly, Iran agrees. A thread of trust forms.

russia_arms_deal.title: RUSSIAN ARMS SHIPMENT
russia_arms_deal.description: Intel shows Russia is shipping advanced anti-ship missiles to Iran. If they're deployed, the strait becomes deadlier.

choices.label: Intercept the shipment
choices.flavor: The ship is turned back. Russia is furious. Iran doesn't get the weapons.

choices.label: Diplomatic protest
choices.flavor: A formal protest. The shipment arrives but world opinion shifts.

choices.label: Ignore it
choices.flavor: The missiles are deployed. Iranian capabilities increase.

iran_internal.title: IRANIAN POWER STRUGGLE
iran_internal.description: Intel suggests moderates within Iran's government are challenging IRGC hardliners. You could tip the balance.

choices.label: Covert support for moderates
choices.flavor: Quiet support shifts the internal debate. IRGC loses influence.

choices.label: Public endorsement
choices.flavor: Your endorsement backfires — moderates are tainted by US association.

choices.label: Stay out of it
choices.flavor: You let Iranian politics play out on their own.

tanker_escort.title: ESCORT CONVOY REQUEST
tanker_escort.description: Multiple shipping companies request military escort through the strait. It would restore confidence but stretch your forces.

choices.label: Full escort operations
choices.flavor: Convoys resume under military escort. Oil markets stabilize.

choices.label: Limited escort for allies only
choices.flavor: Allied flagged ships get protection. Others are on their own.

choices.label: Decline — too risky
choices.flavor: Shipping companies reroute. Costs skyrocket.

wargame_leak.title: WARGAME SIMULATION LEAKED
wargame_leak.description: A Pentagon wargame simulation showing US casualties in a strait conflict was leaked to the press. Public is alarmed.

choices.label: Downplay and investigate leak
choices.flavor: The story dominates the news cycle. The leak investigation goes nowhere.

choices.label: Use it as a warning
choices.flavor: You frame it as evidence for de-escalation. Public opinion shifts toward diplomacy.

choices.label: Double down on readiness
choices.flavor: "We train for worst cases so they never happen." Hawks love it.

stena_imperative.title: TANKER ESCAPE — USS MCFAUL
stena_imperative.description: Six IRGC gunboats are converging on the tanker Stena Imperative. USS McFaul (DDG-74) is 12 minutes away. The tanker captain is requesting emergency escort.

choices.label: Full speed intercept — weapons hot
choices.flavor: McFaul arrives at flank speed. F-35 overhead shoots down an Iranian Shahed drone. IRGC boats scatter. The tanker escapes.

choices.label: Intercept but weapons tight
choices.flavor: McFaul positions between the tanker and gunboats. Tense standoff. IRGC withdraws after 40 minutes.

choices.label: Monitor only — avoid provocation
choices.flavor: IRGC boards and seizes the Stena Imperative. Video goes viral. "Where was the Navy?"

insurance_crisis.title: MARITIME INSURANCE COLLAPSE
insurance_crisis.description: Lloyd's of London and major maritime insurers have cancelled all war risk coverage in the Persian Gulf. No insurance = no tankers transit. Oil markets are panicking.

choices.label: Government-backed insurance program ($20B)
choices.flavor: A $20B government guarantee backstops the market. Tankers resume transit. Chubb signs as lead underwriter.

choices.label: Pressure insurers to resume coverage
choices.flavor: Arm-twisting produces limited coverage at 300% premiums. Some tankers resume.

choices.label: Let the market sort itself out
choices.flavor: Transit drops 70%. 150+ ships anchored outside the strait. Gas hits $6/gallon.

nuclear_breakout.title: NUCLEAR BREAKOUT WARNING
nuclear_breakout.description: IAEA reports Iran has moved enriched uranium to the underground Fordow facility. At 60% enrichment with 440kg stockpile, weapons-grade material is 72 hours away.

choices.label: Strike Fordow — bunker busters
choices.flavor: B-2 bombers drop GBU-57 MOPs on the mountain facility. Iran vows "devastating retaliation." The nuclear clock resets.

choices.label: Emergency UN session — demand inspections
choices.flavor: The Security Council convenes. Russia and China abstain rather than veto. New inspections demanded.

choices.label: Offer down-blend deal
choices.flavor: You offer sanctions relief in exchange for down-blending to 3.67%. Hawks call it appeasement. Iran considers it.

abqaiq_redux.title: SAUDI ARAMCO ATTACK
abqaiq_redux.description: Drone swarm strikes Abqaiq-Khurais oil processing facility. 5.7 million barrels/day knocked offline — half of Saudi production. Oil futures spike 15% overnight.

choices.label: Retaliatory strikes on Iranian launch sites
choices.flavor: Cruise missiles hit IRGC drone bases. Iran denies involvement. The region braces for war.

choices.label: Coordinate with Saudi air defense
choices.flavor: You deploy Patriot batteries and THAAD. Future attacks are intercepted. Production resumes in 10 days.

choices.label: Condemn and investigate
choices.flavor: The investigation drags on. Oil prices stay elevated. Critics ask "What are you waiting for?"

houthi_restart.title: HOUTHI RED SEA CAMPAIGN RESUMES
houthi_restart.description: After months of quiet, Houthi forces launch anti-ship missiles at three commercial vessels in the Red Sea. Container shipping drops 90% through Bab el-Mandeb.

choices.label: Deploy carrier group to Red Sea
choices.flavor: Air strikes degrade Houthi launch sites. But now your forces are split between two theaters.

choices.label: Ask Saudi Arabia to handle it
choices.flavor: Riyadh engages but results are mixed. The Houthis are experienced guerrilla fighters.

choices.label: Focus on the strait — ignore Red Sea
choices.flavor: Shipping reroutes via Cape of Good Hope. 10 extra days, $1M fuel per voyage. Global supply chains buckle.

mine_laying_detected.title: IRAN BEGINS MINING THE STRAIT
mine_laying_detected.description: CNN confirms: Iranian vessels are actively laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz shipping channels. CENTCOM identifies 12+ minelayers operating under cover of darkness.

choices.label: Sink the minelayers
choices.flavor: US forces sink 16 Iranian minelayers in a single night. Iran retains 80% of its small boat fleet.

choices.label: Deploy minesweepers — clear lanes
choices.flavor: Allied minesweepers begin clearing operations. Transit resumes under escort within 72 hours.

choices.label: Establish exclusion zone
choices.flavor: Any vessel in the exclusion zone will be engaged. Iran calls it an act of war.

drone_carrier.title: IRIS SHAHID BAGHERI DETECTED
drone_carrier.description: Intelligence identifies the IRIS Shahid Bagheri — a converted container ship functioning as Iran's first drone carrier. It can deploy 30+ fast attack craft and explosive drone boats from inside the hull.

choices.label: Strike it before it deploys
choices.flavor: Harpoon missiles destroy the vessel. Iran calls it an attack on a "civilian cargo ship." Satellite photos prove otherwise.

choices.label: Track and monitor
choices.flavor: You let it sail, gaining valuable intel on Iran's doctrine. But those drone boats could strike at any time.

choices.label: Leak intel to media
choices.flavor: World media covers the "ghost carrier." Iran forced to dock it. But they know you're watching.

china_oil_deal.title: CHINA'S SHADOW FLEET
china_oil_deal.description: Satellite imagery shows 15 Chinese-flagged tankers loading Iranian oil at Kharg Island. 11.7 million barrels shipped to China since the crisis began. Your sanctions are being openly defied.

choices.label: Secondary sanctions on Chinese banks
choices.flavor: SWIFT cuts off three Chinese banks. Beijing is furious. Iran's revenue collapses. Trade war escalates.

choices.label: Quiet diplomatic pressure
choices.flavor: Beijing makes promises. The shadow fleet shrinks slightly. It's not enough.

choices.label: Look the other way
choices.flavor: Pragmatism over principle. Iran keeps selling. Hawks in Congress are furious.

oman_talks.title: MUSCAT BACK-CHANNEL
oman_talks.description: Omani intermediaries report Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi says a nuclear deal is "within reach." He proposes secret talks in Muscat. Three days later, your intelligence says IRGC is planning a major provocation.

choices.label: Send negotiators to Muscat
choices.flavor: Three hours of talks produce a framework. But is the IRGC on the same page as the Foreign Ministry?

choices.label: Demand preconditions first
choices.flavor: You insist on a tanker release before talks. Iran walks away. The window narrows.

choices.label: It's a distraction — reject
choices.flavor: Hawks applaud. Doves despair. The IRGC provocation happens anyway.

asian_energy_crisis.title: ASIAN ENERGY EMERGENCY
asian_energy_crisis.description: India, Japan, and South Korea declare energy emergencies. 89% of strait oil goes to Asia. India invokes emergency powers to redirect LPG from industry to households. Tokyo warns of rolling blackouts.

choices.label: Coordinate allied reserve release
choices.flavor: IEA coordinates the largest coordinated reserve release in history. Markets stabilize briefly.

choices.label: Prioritize US allies
choices.flavor: Allied tankers get priority escort. India and others are left scrambling.

choices.label: Use as leverage for coalition
choices.flavor: "You want oil? Send warships." Japan and South Korea reluctantly agree to contribute naval assets.

gas_price_crisis.title: GAS HITS $6 PER GALLON
gas_price_crisis.description: Average US gas price breaks $6/gallon. Trucking companies halt routes. Airlines cancel flights. The economic pain is no longer abstract — it's at every pump in America.

choices.label: Emergency SPR release + price cap
choices.flavor: Gas drops to $5.20. Temporary relief. But the reserve is running low.

choices.label: Blame Iran — rally patriotism
choices.flavor: "Iran is attacking YOUR wallet." The message resonates but doesn't lower prices.

choices.label: Fast-track domestic drilling
choices.flavor: Environmental groups protest. Production won't increase for months. But it's a signal.

war_powers_vote.title: WAR POWERS RESOLUTION
war_powers_vote.description: Both chambers of Congress have drafted War Powers Resolutions. If passed, your military options are severely constrained. The vote is in 48 hours.

choices.label: Lobby to defeat it
choices.flavor: Republicans rally. The resolution fails narrowly in the Senate. Democrats vow to try again.

choices.label: Declassify intel to justify actions
choices.flavor: Senators see the classified briefing. The resolution is tabled. But Iran now knows what you know.

choices.label: Accept constraints — show respect for process
choices.flavor: Critics call it weakness. Allies call it maturity. Your military options narrow but legitimacy grows.

tanker_war_echoes.title: OPERATION EARNEST WILL 2.0
tanker_war_echoes.description: Pentagon proposes a formal convoy escort operation through the strait — echoing the 1987-88 Tanker War. Reflagging allied tankers under US flag for legal protection.

choices.label: Launch Earnest Will 2.0
choices.flavor: Reflagged tankers sail under the Stars and Stripes. Any attack is now an attack on America. Oil markets respond.

choices.label: Coalition-led escort (share the burden)
choices.flavor: UK, France, and Australia contribute escorts. Slower to organize but spreads the risk.

choices.label: Too provocative — decline
choices.flavor: Shipping companies continue rerouting via Cape of Good Hope. Transit times and costs skyrocket.

cyber_port_attack.title: IRANIAN CYBER ATTACK — FUJAIRAH PORT
cyber_port_attack.description: APT33 (IRGC-affiliated) has hit Fujairah port systems with wiper malware. Terminal operating systems are down. 40+ tankers can't load or unload. It's the digital equivalent of mining the strait.

choices.label: Counter-cyber operation — hit Iranian ports
choices.flavor: NSA retaliates. Bandar Abbas port goes dark for 72 hours. Cyber escalation spiral begins.

choices.label: Restore systems — defensive posture
choices.flavor: US Cyber Command deploys incident response teams. Systems back online in 5 days. Vulnerability patched.

choices.label: Public attribution and sanctions
choices.flavor: You name and shame APT33 operators. Sanctions on IRGC Cyber Command. Allies express solidarity.

second_carrier.title: SECOND CARRIER DEPLOYMENT
second_carrier.description: CENTCOM requests deployment of a second carrier strike group — USS Gerald R. Ford. It would be the largest naval buildup since 2003. The signal is unmistakable.

choices.label: Deploy the Ford — show maximum force
choices.flavor: Two carrier strike groups in the Gulf. Iran's navy stays in port. The world hasn't seen this since Iraq.

choices.label: Position Ford in Arabian Sea (standoff)
choices.flavor: Close enough to matter, far enough to avoid provocation. A balanced signal.

choices.label: Deny the request — one carrier is enough
choices.flavor: CENTCOM is unhappy but complies. The Ford stays in the Mediterranean.

truth_social_armada.title: "MASSIVE ARMADA HEADING TO IRAN"
truth_social_armada.description: The President posts on Truth Social: "A massive Armada is heading to Iran. They will learn!" The post has 50 million views. Iran's Supreme Leader responds within the hour.

choices.label: Double down — back it with action
choices.flavor: You mobilize visibly. Iran calls it bluffing until the first carrier arrives. Then they go quiet.

choices.label: Walk it back through spokespersons
choices.flavor: "The President was speaking metaphorically." Nobody buys it. Iran smells weakness.

choices.label: Use the chaos as cover for diplomacy
choices.flavor: While the world watches the tweet storm, back-channel talks advance quietly.

iran_internal_struggle.title: IRANIAN MODERATES VS IRGC
iran_internal_struggle.description: Iran's civilian government and IRGC are openly clashing. The Foreign Ministry wants negotiations. The IRGC is planning more seizures. Your intel shows the split is real.

choices.label: Covert support for moderates
choices.flavor: Quiet channels funnel support. The moderates gain ground in internal debates. IRGC funding gets questioned.

choices.label: Tighten sanctions — squeeze both sides
choices.flavor: Maximum pressure doesn't discriminate. The moderates are weakened along with the IRGC.

choices.label: Public overture to moderates
choices.flavor: Your public endorsement backfires. Moderates are labeled American puppets. IRGC hardens.

fast_boat_swarm.title: IRGC FAST BOAT SWARM
fast_boat_swarm.description: TWENTY-FIVE Heydar-class fast boats — the fastest combat boats on earth at 110 knots — are converging on a tanker convoy. This is Iran's signature tactic: overwhelm with speed and numbers.

choices.label: Weapons free — engage the swarm
choices.flavor: Phalanx CIWS and .50 cal fire. Eight boats destroyed. The rest scatter. First naval combat since 1988.

choices.label: Warning shots and countermeasures
choices.flavor: Flares, warning shots, and acoustic devices. The swarm breaks off. For now.

choices.label: Evasive maneuvers — protect the convoy
choices.flavor: The convoy scatters. One tanker is boarded. "Why didn't we shoot?" headlines dominate.

hezbollah_front.title: HEZBOLLAH OPENS SECOND FRONT
hezbollah_front.description: Hezbollah launches missiles and drones into northern Israel from Lebanon. Iran's proxy war is expanding beyond the Gulf. Israel demands US support.

choices.label: Support Israel — provide intel and munitions
choices.flavor: US munitions flow to Israel. Hezbollah positions are degraded. But the US is now fighting on two fronts.

choices.label: Call for ceasefire through UN
choices.flavor: The UN resolution passes. Hezbollah pauses. But everyone knows it's temporary.

choices.label: Stay out — this is Israel's fight
choices.flavor: Israel is disappointed but capable. The conflict expands without direct US involvement.

cape_route_crisis.title: CAPE OF GOOD HOPE BOTTLENECK
cape_route_crisis.description: With the strait effectively closed, 150+ tankers are rerouting via the Cape of Good Hope — adding 11,000 nautical miles, 10 days, and $1M fuel per voyage. Ports in South Africa are overwhelmed.

choices.label: Subsidize rerouting costs
choices.flavor: Government subsidies keep tankers moving. It's expensive but it works.

choices.label: Demand strait reopening as red line
choices.flavor: A formal ultimatum. Iran has 72 hours to guarantee safe passage. The clock ticks.

choices.label: Accept the new normal
choices.flavor: Markets price in the permanent disruption. The "new Suez" route becomes standard. Costs cascade through the global economy.

minab_school_strike.title: MINAB SCHOOL STRIKE
minab_school_strike.description: Reports emerge of a US airstrike hitting a school in Minab, southern Iran. 47 civilians reported killed including children. Iran broadcasting footage globally. UN demands investigation.

choices.label: Express regret — open investigation
choices.flavor: You acknowledge the tragedy. The investigation finds mixed-use targeting. International pressure eases slightly.

choices.label: Deny — claim it was a military site
choices.flavor: Pentagon releases grainy satellite photos. Nobody is convinced. Global protests erupt.

choices.label: Pause air operations for review
choices.flavor: A 72-hour pause. Hawks scream. Allies exhale. Iran uses the time to reposition forces.

mojtaba_succession.title: SUPREME LEADER SUCCESSION CRISIS
mojtaba_succession.description: With Khamenei dead, his son Mojtaba is consolidating power with IRGC backing. But the Assembly of Experts is divided. Iran's internal chaos creates both danger and opportunity.

choices.label: Covert ops to empower moderates
choices.flavor: CIA channels support Raisi-faction moderates. The succession battle intensifies. IRGC is distracted.

choices.label: Publicly demand democratic transition
choices.flavor: Your call for democracy rallies Western opinion but unites Iranian factions against external meddling.

choices.label: Exploit the chaos — escalate strikes
choices.flavor: You hit command centers while leadership is in disarray. Effective but brutal. The world recoils.

joe_kent_resignation.title: NSA KENT RESIGNS
joe_kent_resignation.description: National Security Advisor Joe Kent has resigned, citing disagreements over escalation. He goes on Fox News within the hour. "The President is being misled by hawks who've never seen combat."

choices.label: Attack Kent publicly
choices.flavor: A public feud dominates the news cycle. Your war cabinet looks fractured.

choices.label: Replace quietly — project unity
choices.flavor: The new NSA takes over smoothly. But Kent's book deal is already signed.

choices.label: Reach out — bring him back with concessions
choices.flavor: Kent returns with a mandate for restraint. Hawks are furious but the team is reunited.

gcc_attacks.title: GCC BASES UNDER FIRE
gcc_attacks.description: Iran launches ballistic missiles at Al Udeid (Qatar) and Al Dhafra (UAE). Both house US forces. Patriot batteries intercept most but three impact inside Al Udeid. 8 US casualties.

choices.label: Massive retaliation — hit IRGC bases
choices.flavor: B-2s and Tomahawks devastate IRGC coastal bases. Iran's ability to project force is degraded. But the war deepens.

choices.label: Proportional response — missile batteries only
choices.flavor: Precision strikes eliminate the launch sites. Measured but firm. Allies approve.

choices.label: Evacuate non-essential personnel
choices.flavor: You pull back from exposed positions. Critics call it retreat. Families of the fallen demand action.

iris_dena_aftermath.title: IRIS DENA WRECKAGE
iris_dena_aftermath.description: The Iranian frigate IRIS Dena was sunk on Day 1. Now bodies and wreckage are washing up on Omani beaches. Al Jazeera is broadcasting it live. International pressure mounts.

choices.label: Express regret — offer humanitarian assistance
choices.flavor: A rare gesture of humanity in wartime. Some call it weakness. Others call it leadership.

choices.label: Blame Iran for putting sailors in harm's way
choices.flavor: The messaging works at home. Internationally, it falls flat.

choices.label: No comment — focus on operations
choices.flavor: The silence speaks volumes. The story dominates for 48 hours.

three_carriers.title: THREE CARRIER GROUPS IN THEATER
three_carriers.description: Lincoln, Truman, and Carl Vinson are all in the Arabian Sea. 200+ aircraft, 30 warships, 25,000 sailors. The largest US naval deployment since Iraq 2003. The world is watching.

choices.label: Launch sustained air campaign
choices.flavor: Operation Southern Watch II begins. 200 sorties per day. Iran's military infrastructure is systematically degraded.

choices.label: Maintain pressure — deterrence posture
choices.flavor: The carriers patrol. Iran's navy stays in port. The mere presence changes the calculus.

choices.label: Withdraw one carrier — signal restraint
choices.flavor: Vinson returns to the Pacific. A signal that you're not seeking war. Allies approve. Iran is uncertain.

china_mediation_real.title: BEIJING CEASEFIRE PROPOSAL
china_mediation_real.description: Chinese FM Wang Yi proposes a 5-point ceasefire framework: mutual de-escalation, UN peacekeepers in strait, sanctions relief timeline, nuclear talks restart, regional security forum. Beijing is serious.

choices.label: Accept the framework as starting point
choices.flavor: Talks begin in Beijing. Hawks rage about "selling out to China." But the guns go quiet for the first time in weeks.

choices.label: Counter-propose (US-led framework)
choices.flavor: You reject Chinese mediation but propose your own plan. Beijing is annoyed but the diplomatic track opens.

choices.label: Reject — China is not a neutral broker
choices.flavor: Hawks applaud. China retaliates with arms sales to Iran. The diplomatic window narrows.

russia_intel_leak.title: RUSSIAN INTELLIGENCE LEAK
russia_intel_leak.description: An FSB defector reveals Russia has been sharing US military intelligence with Iran — satellite passes, carrier positions, submarine locations. Your operational security is compromised.

choices.label: Expel Russian diplomats
choices.flavor: 15 diplomats expelled. NATO allies follow suit. Russia retaliates but the leak is plugged.

choices.label: Exploit the defector — feed disinformation
choices.flavor: You turn the leak into an advantage. False intelligence flows to Tehran through Moscow. Iran chases ghosts.

choices.label: Quiet protest — preserve the channel
choices.flavor: A stern demarche. Russia denies everything. But the intelligence sharing slows.

nowruz_ceasefire.title: NOWRUZ CEASEFIRE WINDOW
nowruz_ceasefire.description: Iranian New Year (Nowruz) is in 3 days. Iranian moderates propose a 5-day ceasefire for the holiday. IRGC hasn't objected. This could be the off-ramp.

choices.label: Accept — extend to 10 days
choices.flavor: Both sides stand down. Tankers transit. The ceasefire holds. Diplomats work feverishly to make it permanent.

choices.label: Accept 5 days only
choices.flavor: A brief respite. Oil flows. Markets rally. But everyone knows the clock is ticking.

choices.label: Reject — no pause while tankers are held
choices.flavor: You demand tanker release as precondition. Iran calls you unreasonable. The fighting continues through Nowruz.

hezbollah_ceasefire_break.title: HEZBOLLAH BREAKS CEASEFIRE
hezbollah_ceasefire_break.description: Hezbollah launches 150 rockets into northern Israel, breaking the Lebanon ceasefire. Israel retaliates immediately. Iran's proxy network is activating across the region.

choices.label: Support Israel — joint strikes on Hezbollah
choices.flavor: US and Israeli jets hit Hezbollah positions. The proxy front collapses. But you're now fighting a multi-front war.

choices.label: Demand ceasefire restoration through UN
choices.flavor: French-led mediation produces a fragile truce. Hezbollah pulls back. For now.

choices.label: Focus on strait — Israel can handle it
choices.flavor: You keep your eyes on the prize. Israel is capable. But the multi-front pressure on Iran lessens.

houthi_escalation.title: HOUTHIS TARGET US WARSHIP
houthi_escalation.description: Houthi anti-ship ballistic missile targets USS Gravely (DDG-107) in the Red Sea. SM-2 intercept at 8nm. The closest a Houthi missile has come to hitting a US warship.

choices.label: Massive strike on Houthi infrastructure
choices.flavor: B-2s flatten Houthi missile storage. The Red Sea calms. Yemen civilians pay the price.

choices.label: Tighten the naval screen
choices.flavor: More AEGIS destroyers deployed. An expensive but defensive solution.

choices.label: Pressure Saudi to negotiate with Houthis
choices.flavor: Riyadh reopens the Yemen peace track. Slow but addresses root causes.

kc135_crash.title: KC-135 CRASH — COMBAT LOSSES MOUNT
kc135_crash.description: A KC-135 aerial refueling tanker crashes during combat operations, killing all 3 crew. It's the first US aircraft loss. Cable news shows the wreckage on loop.

choices.label: Honor the fallen — steel resolve
choices.flavor: A solemn ceremony at Dover. The nation mourns but rallies. "Their sacrifice will not be in vain."

choices.label: Review operations — reduce tempo
choices.flavor: Sortie rates drop 40%. Safety improves. Hawks worry about losing momentum.

choices.label: Escalate — make their sacrifice count
choices.flavor: The loss fuels a surge. More targets struck. The cycle accelerates.

war_crimes_tribunal.title: ICC WAR CRIMES INVESTIGATION
war_crimes_tribunal.description: The International Criminal Court announces an investigation into both US and Iranian conduct. Warrants could be issued for senior officials. European allies are divided.

choices.label: Reject ICC jurisdiction — sanction the court
choices.flavor: America First crowd loves it. Europeans are horrified. The legal threat remains for allied officials.

choices.label: Cooperate partially — show good faith
choices.flavor: You provide limited documentation. It buys goodwill. The investigation slows.

choices.label: Use it as leverage against Iran
choices.flavor: You frame it as mutual accountability. Iran's crimes are documented alongside yours. A diplomatic tool emerges.

mojtaba_consolidation.title: MOJTABA KHAMENEI POWER GRAB
mojtaba_consolidation.description: Intelligence confirms Mojtaba Khamenei — the dead Supreme Leader's son — has consolidated IRGC support. He's demanding revenge strikes and threatening to close the strait permanently.

choices.label: Offer to recognize new leadership in exchange for de-escalation
choices.flavor: A bitter pill. Recognizing Khamenei's son feels like losing. But the back-channel opens.

choices.label: Target Mojtaba with a kill order
choices.flavor: Decapitation strike 2.0. If you miss, it's war. If you hit, there's nobody left to negotiate with.

choices.label: Ignore him — focus on the military threat
choices.flavor: You treat the political question as irrelevant. The IRGC reads this as weakness.

al_udeid_attack.title: AL UDEID BASE UNDER FIRE
al_udeid_attack.description: Iranian ballistic missiles hit Al Udeid Air Base in Qatar — the forward HQ of CENTCOM. 3 US personnel killed, 22 wounded. Qatar is demanding you relocate operations.

choices.label: Retaliatory strike on launch site
choices.flavor: Tomahawks fly within 20 minutes. The base is avenged. But the escalation ladder climbs.

choices.label: Relocate to Al Dhafra (UAE)
choices.flavor: Expensive but pragmatic. UAE welcomes the business. Qatar is relieved.

choices.label: Harden defenses and stay
choices.flavor: THAAD batteries deployed. The troops feel exposed. Morale drops.

indian_energy_crisis.title: INDIA ENERGY EMERGENCY
indian_energy_crisis.description: India declares a national energy emergency. They import 80% of their oil through the Strait. PM Modi is calling — demanding action or they will negotiate directly with Iran.

choices.label: Promise priority escort for Indian tankers
choices.flavor: Indian-flagged tankers get USN escorts. Modi is grateful. Other nations want the same deal.

choices.label: Support India-Iran bilateral talks
choices.flavor: India opens a line to Tehran. Hawks accuse you of outsourcing diplomacy.

choices.label: Tell India to wait
choices.flavor: Modi goes public with his frustration. India begins rationing. Blame falls on you.

carrier_near_miss.title: USS EISENHOWER NEAR MISS
carrier_near_miss.description: An Iranian anti-ship ballistic missile splashes 200 meters from the USS Eisenhower. The closest a hostile weapon has come to a US carrier since WWII. The crew is shaken.

choices.label: Destroy Iran's coastal missile batteries
choices.flavor: Operation Burning Light. 48 cruise missiles eliminate Iran's anti-ship capability. The carrier group is safe. For now.

choices.label: Pull the carrier back to the Gulf of Oman
choices.flavor: Retreat. The IRGC celebrates. Your Navy commanders are furious.

choices.label: Stay on station — increase air defense posture
choices.flavor: Aegis systems go to maximum alert. The next shot might not miss.

iran_moderate_coup.title: MODERATE FACTION REACHES OUT
iran_moderate_coup.description: An encrypted message through Swiss channels: Iran's moderate faction is planning to sideline the IRGC hardliners. They need 72 hours — and a gesture of good faith.

choices.label: Pause operations for 72 hours
choices.flavor: Radio silence. The moderate faction moves. Three IRGC commanders are placed under house arrest. A breakthrough.

choices.label: Give them intel on hardliner positions
choices.flavor: Sharing intelligence with a foreign faction. If this leaks, it's an international scandal. But it works.

choices.label: This is a trap — intensify operations
choices.flavor: You don't trust it. Operations continue. The moderate window closes.

kc135_crew_rescue.title: DOWNED KC-135 CREW
kc135_crew_rescue.description: The KC-135 tanker shot down on Day 1 — search teams have located 2 surviving crew members on an Iranian island. IRGC is closing in.

choices.label: SEAL team rescue — extract at all costs
choices.flavor: Spec ops infiltrate under cover of darkness. Both Americans rescued alive. The nation rallies.

choices.label: Negotiate through Oman
choices.flavor: Slow, agonizing negotiations. The crew becomes a bargaining chip. Families go on CNN.

choices.label: Deny the crew exists — classify everything
choices.flavor: The cover-up doesn't hold. When the truth emerges, the fallout is devastating.

european_split.title: NATO FRACTURES
european_split.description: France breaks with the US — Macron calls the Iran strikes "disproportionate" and blocks NATO solidarity statement. Germany is wavering. The UK stands with you — for now.

choices.label: Bilateral with the UK — bypass NATO
choices.flavor: The "special relationship" holds. But NATO is weaker than it's been since Suez.

choices.label: Offer Europe a seat at the negotiating table
choices.flavor: Macron gets his summit. NATO cohesion is preserved. The diplomatic path opens wider.

choices.label: Publicly shame France
choices.flavor: "Freedom fries" is trending. The base loves it. Diplomats are horrified.

backchannel_progress.title: BACK-CHANNEL UPDATE
backchannel_progress.description: Your Omani intermediary reports back. The Iranian delegation showed up — a significant signal. They presented a list of demands: sanctions relief on medical supplies, return of frozen assets, and a public statement acknowledging Iranian sovereignty. In exchange, they offer a 48-hour shipping ceasefire and the release of 4 detained crew members.

choices.label: Accept the ceasefire deal
choices.flavor: Both sides stand down. For the first time in weeks, the strait is quiet. But hawks on both sides are furious.

choices.label: Counter-offer: ceasefire first, then talks
choices.flavor: A reasonable position. The Omanis carry it back. Now you wait.

choices.label: Reject — these terms are weakness
choices.flavor: The channel goes dark. Iran's moderates lose face. The hardliners say "we told you so."

backchannel_ceasefire_test.title: CEASEFIRE UNDER PRESSURE
backchannel_ceasefire_test.description: Day two of the ceasefire. IRGC fast boats are still patrolling — technically not a violation, but provocative. Then: an IRGC commander's nephew is caught planting a limpet mine on a Kuwaiti-flagged tanker. Iran claims he acted alone. Your military advisors want to respond. The Omani mediator begs you not to.

choices.label: Hold the ceasefire — accept Iran's explanation
choices.flavor: You absorb the political cost. But the ceasefire holds. Oil begins to flow. The Omanis call it "a brave choice."

choices.label: Demand Iran arrest the commander publicly
choices.flavor: Iran refuses. The ceasefire technically holds but trust is damaged. Both sides are watching for the next provocation.

choices.label: Strike the IRGC base — ceasefire is over
choices.flavor: Tomahawks hit Bandar Abbas. The ceasefire is dead. Iran's moderates are silenced for a generation.

backchannel_counteroffer_response.title: IRAN'S RESPONSE
backchannel_counteroffer_response.description: The Omani intermediary returns. Iran's counter-counter-proposal: they'll reduce IRGC patrols by 50% for 72 hours as a "gesture of goodwill," but no formal ceasefire. They want reciprocity — pull one carrier group back to show good faith. The moderates are sticking their necks out. This is their last offer.

choices.label: Accept — pull a carrier back
choices.flavor: USS Truman moves to the Arabian Sea. IRGC boats thin out. A fragile de-escalation begins.

choices.label: Agree to patrol reduction but keep carriers
choices.flavor: A half-measure that satisfies neither side fully. But the shooting stops, for now.

choices.label: Reject — Iran must concede first
choices.flavor: The channel collapses. The Omani ambassador says "we tried." Iran's moderates won't recover from this.

hostage_situation_escalates.title: HOSTAGE CRISIS DEEPENS
hostage_situation_escalates.description: Three of the 12 detained crew members appear on Iranian state TV, reading prepared statements calling for America to "stop its aggression." Intelligence suggests they're being held at Evin Prison, not a military facility — a signal Iran wants to negotiate. The families are on CNN demanding action. A retired admiral is calling for a rescue operation on Fox News.

choices.label: Send a private envoy to Tehran
choices.flavor: A former diplomat boards a flight to Muscat, then Tehran. Total media blackout. If this leaks, it's a political disaster.

choices.label: Authorize a rescue operation
choices.flavor: Delta Force operators begin mission planning. The risk is enormous. If it fails, you own it.

choices.label: Impose personal sanctions on IRGC commanders
choices.flavor: A measured response that satisfies nobody fully. The families are disappointed. Iran is unmoved.

hostage_envoy_result.title: THE ENVOY REPORTS
hostage_envoy_result.description: Your envoy spent 72 hours in Tehran. The news is mixed. Iran will release 8 of the 12 crew — but they're keeping 4 "for investigation into espionage." The price: unfreeze $2 billion in Iranian assets and issue a joint statement about "mutual respect for maritime sovereignty." The envoy says the moderates were sincere but scared. The IRGC was listening to every word.

choices.label: Accept the partial release
choices.flavor: 8 crew members land at Ramstein. The families cry on camera. But 4 remain. The opposition calls it "paying ransom."

choices.label: All 12 or no deal
choices.flavor: You draw the line. Iran goes silent. The 4 are moved to an unknown location.

hostage_rescue_result.title: OPERATION EAGLE CLAW II
hostage_rescue_result.description: The rescue force launched at 0200 local time. Two MH-60 Black Hawks, 24 operators, supported by AC-130 gunships. They reached Evin Prison and breached the facility. But Iranian intelligence was waiting.

choices.label: Read the after-action report
choices.flavor: 3 operators KIA. 2 hostages killed in crossfire. 6 rescued. Iran parades the wreckage on TV. The world recoils. This is your Desert One.

mojtaba_power_play.title: MOJTABA CONSOLIDATES
mojtaba_power_play.description: Mojtaba Khamenei has purged three senior IRGC commanders who opposed his succession. Satellite imagery shows Republican Guard units redeploying to Tehran — not to the Gulf. Intelligence suggests he's more afraid of internal rivals than of you. A CIA source inside the Guardian Council reports he might be open to a deal if it helps him consolidate power.

choices.label: Reach out through CIA channels
choices.flavor: Your message reaches Mojtaba through three cutouts. He doesn't respond — but he doesn't shut it down either.

choices.label: Exploit the chaos — increase pressure
choices.flavor: While Iran fights itself, you tighten the noose. Sanctions hit harder when the government is distracted.

choices.label: Publicly support Iranian moderates
choices.flavor: Your speech is played on BBC Persian. Iranian moderates are now accused of being American agents. You meant well.

mojtaba_secret_deal.title: THE SUPREME LEADER'S OFFER
mojtaba_secret_deal.description: Mojtaba's people made contact. The proposal is extraordinary: Iran will reopen the strait and halt enrichment above 20% — permanently. In exchange, they want recognition of the new Supreme Leader, removal of all personal sanctions on Mojtaba, and a secret $5 billion reconstruction fund. The CIA says he's genuine. The risk: if Congress finds out, it's Iran-Contra 2.0.

choices.label: Accept the grand bargain
choices.flavor: You make a deal with the devil you don't know. The strait opens. Oil flows. But the secret eats at you. One leak and it's over.

choices.label: Accept partially — strait for sanctions relief only
choices.flavor: Mojtaba gets less than he wanted but more than he had. The strait reopens gradually. Both sides claim victory.

choices.label: Report to Congress and decline
choices.flavor: You do the right thing. Congress applauds your transparency. Iran's moderates collapse. Mojtaba becomes a hardliner overnight.

nuclear_inspection_crisis.title: IAEA DEMANDS ACCESS
nuclear_inspection_crisis.description: Following your push for international inspection, the IAEA team arrived at Fordow — and was turned away. Iran claims "construction" prevents access. Satellite imagery shows new tunneling activity. The IAEA chief calls you directly: "They're 2-3 weeks from enough material for a device. Maybe less." Israel is moving Jericho missiles to launch positions.

choices.label: Coordinate strikes with Israel on Fordow
choices.flavor: Bunker busters penetrate 80 meters of rock. Fordow is destroyed. Iran vows revenge "for a thousand years." The nuclear program is set back 5 years — or 5 months.

choices.label: Threaten strikes unless Iran admits inspectors
choices.flavor: An ultimatum with teeth. The world watches.

choices.label: Propose a new deal — enrichment caps for sanctions relief
choices.flavor: Critics call it "JCPOA 2.0." Supporters call it "the only path that doesn't end in mushroom clouds."

nuclear_ultimatum_response.title: IRAN'S 72-HOUR RESPONSE
nuclear_ultimatum_response.description: The deadline passes. Iran's response comes not through diplomats but through actions: they move centrifuge cascades to a new, unknown facility. Meanwhile, Mojtaba appears on television and says, "Iran will never bow to nuclear blackmail — we seek peaceful energy, and we will defend our rights." The IAEA says they've lost track of 8kg of enriched uranium.

choices.label: Launch strikes on all known nuclear sites
choices.flavor: Operation Olympic Games. B-2s from Whiteman, Tomahawks from the Gulf. 47 targets in one night. The region will never be the same.

choices.label: Accept the new reality — contain and deter
choices.flavor: You choose containment over war. History will judge whether this was wisdom or cowardice.

assassination_crisis.title: [ IMMINENT THREAT ]
assassination_crisis.description: Multiple intelligence agencies confirm a credible, imminent assassination plot. You have minutes to decide.

choices.label: Full lockdown — cancel all public appearances
choices.flavor: You go underground. The threat is neutralized but you look weak.

choices.label: Counter-strike on the plotters
choices.flavor: Special forces neutralize the cell. The message is clear.

choices.label: Ignore it — show strength
choices.flavor: You appear in public defiantly. The risk remains.

nuclear_threshold.title: [ NUCLEAR THRESHOLD ]
nuclear_threshold.description: IAEA reports Iran at 90% enrichment with 440kg stockpile. Israel is on the phone. "We strike in 12 hours unless you act." Russia warns any strike will be treated as aggression against their ally.

choices.label: Green-light Israeli strike
choices.flavor: Israeli F-35s hit Fordow and Natanz. Iran vows "devastating retaliation." Russia recalls ambassador. The nuclear clock resets but the war clock accelerates.

choices.label: US strike — bunker busters on Fordow
choices.flavor: B-2 bombers drop GBU-57 MOPs. The mountain facility is destroyed. You own this escalation now.

choices.label: Emergency UN session — buy time
choices.flavor: The Security Council convenes. Israel fumes. Iran enriches. You bought 72 hours — at most.

choices.label: Call Tehran directly
choices.flavor: A stunning phone call. Iran agrees to freeze enrichment for 30 days. Hawks call it "the worst deal since Munich." But the bombs stay in their bays.

choices.label: Do nothing — call the bluff
choices.flavor: Israel strikes alone 11 hours later. The fallout — literal and political — is now your problem without the credit.

three_seizures.title: [ MASS SEIZURE EVENT ]
three_seizures.description: IRGC Navy seizes THREE tankers simultaneously — Norwegian, Japanese, and Greek-flagged. 67 hostages. This is not a provocation. This is a coordinated operation. The world is watching your response in real time.

choices.label: Immediate military rescue operation
choices.flavor: SEALs board two tankers. The third is too far. 4 hostages wounded, 1 killed. Iran calls it piracy. Fox News calls you a hero.

choices.label: Naval blockade of Iranian ports
choices.flavor: "Nothing enters or leaves Iranian waters until our people are free." A classic escalation. Effective but the world economy shudders.

choices.label: Hostage negotiation through Oman
choices.flavor: Negotiations begin. Iran demands sanctions relief. Day 1 passes. Day 2. The families are on every channel. "WHERE ARE YOU?"

choices.label: Authorize "all necessary force"
choices.flavor: Congress grants authorization. The US is now legally at war with Iran in all but name.

friendly_fire.title: [ FRIENDLY FIRE INCIDENT ]
friendly_fire.description: USS Vella Gulf (CG-72) has engaged and destroyed a vessel in the strait. It was an Emirati coast guard cutter. 11 UAE personnel killed. The UAE ambassador is on the phone. Al Jazeera has the footage.

choices.label: Immediate public apology + compensation
choices.flavor: You own it immediately. $500M compensation. The UAE accepts but Al Udeid base access is "under review." Trust is damaged.

choices.label: Blame fog of war — express regret
choices.flavor: "In the fog of war, terrible mistakes happen." The UAE pulls their ambassador. Gulf coalition fractures.

choices.label: Classify the incident — suppress footage
choices.flavor: The cover-up lasts 36 hours before a sailor leaks the bridge recording. Now it's a scandal AND a tragedy.

choices.label: Stand down all operations for 48 hours
choices.flavor: A full operational pause. Iran exploits the gap. But no more mistakes. The Navy resets its ROE.

cascade_crisis.title: [ CASCADE FAILURE ]
cascade_crisis.description: Houthi anti-ship missile hits LNG tanker in Bab el-Mandeb. Tanker is on fire, crew abandoning ship. Simultaneously, IRGC mines detonate under a VLCC in Hormuz. AND Iraqi militias are rocketing Al Asad. Three theaters. One response.

choices.label: Prioritize Hormuz — it's the jugular
choices.flavor: You save the VLCC crew and clear the mines. The LNG tanker burns. Al Asad takes hits. You can't be everywhere.

choices.label: Retaliate everywhere — show no weakness
choices.flavor: Tomahawks hit all three fronts simultaneously. Iran's proxy network is degraded. Your budget is cratered. The region is on fire.

choices.label: Call emergency NATO Article 5
choices.flavor: First Article 5 invocation since 9/11. NATO responds — eventually. The bureaucracy buys Iran 72 hours to dig in.

choices.label: Emergency ceasefire offer to Iran
choices.flavor: You offer to stop all strikes if Iran reins in proxies. Iran says yes. Then the Houthis fire again the next day. "They don't control their own proxies."

carrier_hit.title: [ CARRIER UNDER ATTACK ]
carrier_hit.description: FLASH: USS Abraham Lincoln reports missile impact. Iranian anti-ship ballistic missile. Flight deck damaged. 23 casualties. The carrier is NOT sinking but air operations are suspended. This is the first hit on a US carrier since WWII.

choices.label: Massive retaliation — destroy Iran's missile capability
choices.flavor: 200 Tomahawks in a single salvo. Every known missile site hit. Iran's offensive capability is shattered. The war is now total.

choices.label: Proportional response — hit the launch site
choices.flavor: Cruise missiles destroy the specific battery that fired. Measured. Professional. But 23 families want more.

choices.label: Withdraw the Lincoln — save the crew
choices.flavor: The Lincoln limps to Fujairah. Iran celebrates. "We drove the Americans from our waters." Your deterrence is in ruins.

choices.label: Invoke Article II — address the nation
choices.flavor: "23 Americans were attacked today. We will respond at a time and manner of our choosing." The nation rallies. Iran waits.

escalationLadder.name: DIPLOMATIC TENSIONS
escalationLadder.description: Sanctions and rhetoric. No military engagement.

escalationLadder.name: NAVAL STANDOFF
escalationLadder.description: Warships deployed. Seizures and standoffs. Limited skirmishes.

escalationLadder.name: LIMITED STRIKES
escalationLadder.description: Precision strikes on military targets. Tit-for-tat.

escalationLadder.name: AIR CAMPAIGN
escalationLadder.description: Sustained air operations. Infrastructure targeted. Civilian risk.

escalationLadder.name: GROUND INVASION
escalationLadder.description: Ground forces deployed. Draft considered. Regional war.

escalationLadder.name: TOTAL WAR
escalationLadder.description: Nuclear threshold. Chemical weapons. Civilization at stake.

the_spark.name: THE SPARK
the_spark.brief: You inherit a crisis in motion. Nobody expects you to last.

the_squeeze.name: THE SQUEEZE
the_squeeze.brief: Iran tightens. A second vessel seized. Your opposition smells blood.

diplomatic_window.name: THE DIPLOMATIC WINDOW
diplomatic_window.brief: A brief thaw. Iran releases crew. The UN convenes. Choose wisely.

false_dawn.name: THE FALSE DAWN
false_dawn.brief: Whatever progress you made gets complicated. Every path leads to a setback.

proxy_season.name: PROXY SEASON
proxy_season.brief: Houthis. Hezbollah. Iraqi militias. The crisis is metastasizing.

pressure_cooker.name: THE PRESSURE COOKER
pressure_cooker.brief: Congressional hearings. Leaked documents. Iran issues an ultimatum.

crossroads.name: CROSSROADS
crossroads.brief: The ultimatum expires. What happens depends on what you've built.

the_grind.name: THE GRIND
the_grind.brief: The world adjusts. The crisis becomes chronic. Fatigue sets in.

endgame_setup.name: ENDGAME
endgame_setup.brief: A breakthrough reveals Iran's true objective. The board reshapes.

resolution.name: RESOLUTION
resolution.brief: Diplomatic resolution, military victory, or managed decline. History is watching.

winLoseReasons.win_strait_open: The Strait of Hormuz has been open and stable for 7 consecutive days. Crisis resolved through [masterful diplomacy / strong leadership / persistent strategy].
winLoseReasons.win_day91_stable: After 13 weeks of crisis management, the situation has stabilized enough for a diplomatic resolution.
winLoseReasons.lose_approval: Congress has initiated removal proceedings. Your approval collapsed to [X]% and stayed there. Your time in the Situation Room is over.
winLoseReasons.lose_assassination_extreme: Despite warnings, the threat was not contained. A sophisticated attack succeeds. The crisis claims its highest-profile casualty.
winLoseReasons.lose_international: The international community has turned against you. Allies recall ambassadors. Sanctions against the United States are being discussed. You are a global pariah.
winLoseReasons.lose_polarization: Domestic unrest has reached a breaking point. The military is split. The crisis abroad has ignited a crisis at home. Civil order collapses.
winLoseReasons.lose_war: Too many unresolved military incidents. The escalation spiral is irreversible. Armed conflict erupts in the Strait of Hormuz. A regional war has begun.
winLoseReasons.lose_day91_unstable: After 13 weeks, the situation remains unstable. You failed to open the strait.

overnightNewsFiller: A quiet day in the strait. Tankers transit normally.
Diplomatic channels remain open. No major incidents.
Markets hold steady. The situation is unchanged.
